solutions.title: Secure AI Platform
solutions.tagline: Zero-trust security for regulated AI deployments
solutions.desc:
A hardened security stack for organizations running AI in regulated environments.
Combines policy enforcement, secrets management, runtime threat detection, and
audit logging to meet SOC 2, HIPAA, and FedRAMP requirements.
useCases: HIPAA-compliant AI workloads in healthcare
FedRAMP-authorized government AI deployments
Financial services AI with PCI DSS requirements
Zero-trust access to ML training infrastructure

components.name: kyverno
components.why: Validates every AI workload manifest against security policies

components.name: falco
components.why: Real-time threat detection for GPU pods and ML pipelines

components.name: external-secrets
components.why: Sync API keys from Vault and AWS securely

components.name: teleport-cluster
components.why: MFA-gated access to AI training clusters with audit logs

components.name: cert-manager
components.why: Automated mTLS between model serving components

components.name: gatekeeper
components.why: OPA policies to enforce GPU resource quotas and labels

solutions.title: AI Data Platform
solutions.tagline: Scalable data infrastructure for AI training pipelines
solutions.desc:
A composable data platform for AI teams needing reliable storage, messaging,
and workflow orchestration for training pipelines. Supports the full data lifecycle
from ingestion to model-ready datasets.
useCases: Training data ingestion and preprocessing pipelines
Feature store for real-time ML serving
Workflow automation for data labeling
Multi-modal data management (text, image, audio)

components.name: minio
components.why: S3-compatible storage for raw datasets and model artifacts

components.name: milvus
components.why: Store and query high-dimensional embeddings at scale

components.name: strimzi-kafka-operator
components.why: Real-time event streaming for continuous ML pipelines

components.name: postgresql
components.why: Relational store for feature metadata and labels

components.name: redis
components.why: Low-latency feature serving for real-time ML inference

components.name: influxdb2
components.why: Metrics and sensor data for IoT and predictive AI models

solutions.title: AI Inference Stack (NVIDIA)
solutions.tagline: NVIDIA GPU-optimized inference serving at enterprise scale
solutions.desc:
A fully integrated stack for deploying and serving LLMs and ML models in production. Combines GPU provisioning, model serving,
observability, and autoscaling into a single validated blueprint for organizations running LLM inference and real-time
prediction workloads.
useCases: Air-gapped enterprise chatbots running private LLMs on local infrastructure
Custom model serving for domain-specific AI assistants and real-time inference workloads
AI image generation and multimodal pipelines with scalable GPU-backed inference

components.name: gpu-operator
components.why: Automates NVIDIA GPU driver and deployment on Kubernetes nodes

components.name: open-webui
components.why: Self-hosted OpenAI-compatible UI for model interaction and testing

components.name: traefik
components.why: Routes external traffic to model serving endpoints with TLS termination

clds.title: Small
clds.subtitle: Basic 2 nodes cluster, dev/test or startup
clds.cld:
apiVersion: k0rdent.mirantis.com/v1beta1
kind: ClusterDeployment
metadata:
  name: aws-demo-cluster
  namespace: kcm-system
  labels:
    type: aws
    group: demo
spec:
  template: aws-standalone-cp-1-0-20
  credential: aws-credential
  config:
    controlPlane:
      instanceType: t3.small
    controlPlaneNumber: 1
    publicIP: false
    region: eu-central-1
    worker:
      # Small AWS instance with NVIDIA GPU
      instanceType: g4dn.xlarge
      # AMI Catalog - Community AMIs:
      #  Find region specific AMI ID with title:
      #  "ubuntu/images/hvm-ssd/ubuntu-jammy-22.04-amd64-server"
      #  eu-central-1: ami-0162f0739222cca1c, us-east-2: ami-00eb69d236edcfaf8, ap-south-1: ami-0b738b0c888af81f7
      amiID: "ami-0162f0739222cca1c"
      imageLookup: {org: "", format: "", baseOS: ""}
      rootVolumeSize: 100
    workersNumber: 1

solutions.title: Distributed Tracing with Tempo and Istio
solutions.tagline: OTLP trace collection, Istio span generation, and Grafana trace exploration
solutions.desc: End-to-end request tracing from ingress through service mesh to backend — visualized in Grafana
useCases: Distributed trace collection across AI serving pipelines
Latency analysis for model inference endpoints
Correlation of traces with metrics and logs in Grafana

components.name: traefik
components.why: Exposes Grafana dashboards and generates traceable HTTP traffic

components.name: tempo
components.why: Stores distributed traces in object storage for Grafana querying

components.name: opentelemetry-collector
components.why: Receives OTLP spans from Istio proxies and forwards to Tempo

components.name: istio-base
components.why: Installs Istio CRDs required for sidecar injection and tracing

components.name: istiod
components.why: Configures Envoy sidecars to emit trace spans via OpenTelemetry

components.name: raw
components.why: Deploys Istio Telemetry CR to configure trace export to OTel collector

components.name: kube-prometheus-stack
components.why: Provides Grafana with Tempo data source for trace visualization

infra.name: aks
infra.title: Azure Kubernetes Service
infra.desc: Microsoft's managed Kubernetes offering. k0rdent deploys and manages service templates on AKS clusters, with native integration for Azure AD, Azure Monitor, and GPU node pools.
infra.description: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of containerized applications in the Azure cloud. k0rdent can create and maage clusters with AKS.
infra.descriptionHtml: Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of containerized applications in the Azure cloud. k0rdent can create and maage clusters with AKS.
infra.whyInCatalog: Carefully selected by Mirantis platform engineers for its production-grade quality, active community, and proven interoperability with k0rdent-managed clusters.

infra.name: ampere
infra.title: Ampere
infra.desc: Maximum performance for GPU-intensive AI workloads. Run directly on physical servers without hypervisor overhead, enabling GPUDirect RDMA, NVLink, and InfiniBand at full hardware capability.
infra.description:
Ampere Altra-based platforms are now supported for use with k0rdent child clusters.
These ARM64-based systems are optimized for highly efficient, scalable computing and are
ideal for edge, telecom, and AI-driven workloads.

Ampere’s architectural design enables running k0rdent child clusters in environments with
power or thermal constraints—such as edge data centers, mobile base stations, or
embedded applications—without sacrificing performance or orchestration capabilities.

Whether deploying traditional Kubernetes services or advanced AI-enhanced applications,
Ampere’s balance of performance-per-watt and deterministic behavior makes it a solid
platform for production-grade, multi-cluster k0rdent workloads.
infra.descriptionHtml:
Ampere Altra-based platforms are now supported for use with k0rdent child clusters. These ARM64-based systems are optimized for highly efficient, scalable computing and are ideal for edge, telecom, and AI-driven workloads.

Ampere’s architectural design enables running k0rdent child clusters in environments with power or thermal constraints—such as edge data centers, mobile base stations, or embedded applications—without sacrificing performance or orchestration capabilities.

Whether deploying traditional Kubernetes services or advanced AI-enhanced applications, Ampere’s balance of performance-per-watt and deterministic behavior makes it a solid platform for production-grade, multi-cluster k0rdent workloads.
infra.whyInCatalog: Carefully selected by Mirantis platform engineers for its production-grade quality, active community, and proven interoperability with k0rdent-managed clusters.

infra.name: aws
infra.title: Amazon EC2
infra.desc: Deploy on self-managed Kubernetes clusters running on Amazon EC2 instances. Supports all instance families including GPU-optimized P4, P5, and G5 instances for AI workloads.
infra.description:
k0rdent, as a multi-cluster Kubernetes management platform, integrates with AWS to provide a seamless experience for managing and deploying Kubernetes clusters and applications on the AWS cloud. Here's how this integration works:

- Cluster Deployment: k0rdent can deploy Kubernetes clusters on AWS using CAPI. 
- Infrastructure Management: k0rdent can provision and manage AWS infrastructure resources, such as EC2 instances, VPCs, and security groups, required for your Kubernetes clusters.
- Centralized Management: Manage your AWS-based Kubernetes clusters from the k0rdent control plane, along with clusters on other platforms.
- Cost Optimization: k0rdent can provide insights into your AWS spending and help you optimize resource allocation for your Kubernetes deployments.
- Security and Compliance: k0rdent integrates with AWS security services to ensure your Kubernetes clusters and applications are secure and compliant with industry standards.
infra.descriptionHtml:
k0rdent, as a multi-cluster Kubernetes management platform, integrates with AWS to provide a seamless experience for managing and deploying Kubernetes clusters and applications on the AWS cloud. Here's how this integration works:

  • Cluster Deployment: k0rdent can deploy Kubernetes clusters on AWS using CAPI.
  • Infrastructure Management: k0rdent can provision and manage AWS infrastructure resources, such as EC2 instances, VPCs, and security groups, required for your Kubernetes clusters.
  • Centralized Management: Manage your AWS-based Kubernetes clusters from the k0rdent control plane, along with clusters on other platforms.
  • Cost Optimization: k0rdent can provide insights into your AWS spending and help you optimize resource allocation for your Kubernetes deployments.
  • Security and Compliance: k0rdent integrates with AWS security services to ensure your Kubernetes clusters and applications are secure and compliant with industry standards.
infra.whyInCatalog: Carefully selected by Mirantis platform engineers for its production-grade quality, active community, and proven interoperability with k0rdent-managed clusters.

infra.name: azure
infra.title: Azure Compute Services
infra.desc: Self-managed Kubernetes on Azure VMs. Supports NC, ND, and NV series GPU instances for AI training and inference workloads alongside standard compute.
infra.description:
K0rdent streamlines the deployment, management, and monitoring of Kubernetes clusters on Microsoft Azure, simplifying container orchestration in the cloud. Here's how it works:

- Automated Cluster Creation: K0rdent provides a user-friendly interface or API to easily provision Kubernetes clusters on Microsoft Azure. Define your desired cluster configuration, including:
	-  Region and availability zones
	-  Virtual machine sizes and node counts
	-  Kubernetes version
	-  Networking options
- Infrastructure as Code: Leverage infrastructure-as-code capabilities to define and manage your Azure infrastructure and Kubernetes deployments in a declarative manner.
- Simplified Configuration: K0rdent handles the complexities of configuring network settings, security policies, and storage integration for your Azure hosted Kubernetes clusters.
infra.descriptionHtml:
K0rdent streamlines the deployment, management, and monitoring of Kubernetes clusters on Microsoft Azure, simplifying container orchestration in the cloud. Here's how it works:

  • Automated Cluster Creation: K0rdent provides a user-friendly interface or API to easily provision Kubernetes clusters on Microsoft Azure. Define your desired cluster configuration, including:
  • Region and availability zones
  • Virtual machine sizes and node counts
  • Kubernetes version
  • Networking options
  • Infrastructure as Code: Leverage infrastructure-as-code capabilities to define and manage your Azure infrastructure and Kubernetes deployments in a declarative manner.
  • Simplified Configuration: K0rdent handles the complexities of configuring network settings, security policies, and storage integration for your Azure hosted Kubernetes clusters.
infra.whyInCatalog: Carefully selected by Mirantis platform engineers for its production-grade quality, active community, and proven interoperability with k0rdent-managed clusters.

infra.name: bare-metal
infra.title: Bare Metal
infra.desc: Maximum performance for GPU-intensive AI workloads. Run directly on physical servers without hypervisor overhead, enabling GPUDirect RDMA, NVLink, and InfiniBand at full hardware capability.
infra.description:
K0rdent streamlines the deployment, management, and monitoring of Kubernetes clusters on bare metal infrastructure,
enabling efficient orchestration of physical servers for cloud-native and AI workloads.
infra.descriptionHtml: K0rdent streamlines the deployment, management, and monitoring of Kubernetes clusters on bare metal infrastructure, enabling efficient orchestration of physical servers for cloud-native and AI workloads.
infra.whyInCatalog: Carefully selected by Mirantis platform engineers for its production-grade quality, flexibility, and proven interoperability with k0rdent-managed clusters.

infra.name: eks
infra.title: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service
infra.desc: Managed Kubernetes on AWS. EKS handles the control plane while k0rdent manages workload deployment across node groups. Ideal for teams that want AWS-managed Kubernetes with k0rdent's multi-cluster orchestration.
infra.description: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) is a managed Kubernetes service offered by AWS that streamlines the deployment, management, and scaling of containerized applications. By abstracting away the complexities of the Kubernetes control plane, EKS allows users to focus on building and deploying their applications while leveraging the robust infrastructure and security of AWS.
infra.descriptionHtml: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) is a managed Kubernetes service offered by AWS that streamlines the deployment, management, and scaling of containerized applications. By abstracting away the complexities of the Kubernetes control plane, EKS allows users to focus on building and deploying their applications while leveraging the robust infrastructure and security of AWS.
infra.whyInCatalog: Carefully selected by Mirantis platform engineers for its production-grade quality, active community, and proven interoperability with k0rdent-managed clusters.

infra.name: gcp
infra.title: Google Compute Engine
infra.desc: Self-managed Kubernetes on GCP virtual machines. Full control over node configuration including A100, H100, and L4 GPU instances, TPU v4 pods, and custom machine types for AI workloads.
infra.description:
K0rdent supports deploying and managing Kubernetes clusters directly on Google Compute Engine (GCE) virtual machines, offering full lifecycle control without relying on GKE. Here’s how k0rdent integrates with Google Cloud’s compute infrastructure:

- Cluster Deployment on GCE: Provision Kubernetes clusters on Google Compute Engine VMs using Cluster API (CAPI) with the Google Cloud infrastructure provider.
- Custom Infrastructure Management: Define and manage your own networking, firewall rules, disks, and VM configurations to tailor clusters to your specific needs—beyond the limitations of GKE.
- Centralized Multi-Cloud Management: Operate your Google Compute-based clusters from the k0rdent control plane alongside other cloud or on-prem clusters.
- Flexible Resource Allocation: Optimize node sizing, regional placement, and scaling strategies with full control over VM-based infrastructure.
- Security and IAM Integration: K0rdent integrates with Google Cloud IAM and supports configuring secure access, firewall rules, and identity-aware access policies for your Kubernetes workloads.
infra.descriptionHtml:
K0rdent supports deploying and managing Kubernetes clusters directly on Google Compute Engine (GCE) virtual machines, offering full lifecycle control without relying on GKE. Here’s how k0rdent integrates with Google Cloud’s compute infrastructure:

  • Cluster Deployment on GCE: Provision Kubernetes clusters on Google Compute Engine VMs using Cluster API (CAPI) with the Google Cloud infrastructure provider.
  • Custom Infrastructure Management: Define and manage your own networking, firewall rules, disks, and VM configurations to tailor clusters to your specific needs—beyond the limitations of GKE.
  • Centralized Multi-Cloud Management: Operate your Google Compute-based clusters from the k0rdent control plane alongside other cloud or on-prem clusters.
  • Flexible Resource Allocation: Optimize node sizing, regional placement, and scaling strategies with full control over VM-based infrastructure.
  • Security and IAM Integration: K0rdent integrates with Google Cloud IAM and supports configuring secure access, firewall rules, and identity-aware access policies for your Kubernetes workloads.
infra.whyInCatalog: Carefully selected by Mirantis platform engineers for its production-grade quality, active community, and proven interoperability with k0rdent-managed clusters.

infra.name: gke
infra.title: Google Kubernetes Engine
infra.desc: Google's managed Kubernetes platform. Deploy k0rdent service templates on GKE clusters including Autopilot and Standard modes, with access to A100, H100, and TPU accelerators.
infra.description:
K0rdent seamlessly integrates with Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE), enabling efficient management of GKE clusters alongside other environments through a centralized control plane. Here’s how k0rdent works with GKE:

- GKE Cluster Integration: Connect and manage existing or newly created GKE clusters using k0rdent, with support for importing clusters and applying consistent management policies.
- Declarative Configuration: Define GKE cluster configurations—such as regions, node pools, autoscaling, and Kubernetes versions—through infrastructure-as-code workflows.
- Multi-Cluster Visibility: Monitor and control GKE clusters from the k0rdent platform alongside clusters from AWS, Azure, on-prem, or other clouds.
- Enhanced Governance and Scaling: Apply policies, automate upgrades, and scale workloads across GKE clusters using k0rdent’s governance and automation features.
- Secure Access and IAM: Leverage Google Cloud IAM and GKE’s built-in security features while applying k0rdent’s role-based access control (RBAC) and policy enforcement across environments.
